name: Ilex Nellie Stevens Multistem
description:
Botanical Name: Ilex × ‘Nellie R. Stevens’
Height Supplied: 175–200cm
Form: Multistem
Quick Facts:
  • Foliage: Glossy, deep green, evergreen
  • Flowers: Tiny white blooms in spring
  • Berries: Abundant, vibrant red in autumn/winter, self pollinating
  • Position: Sun or part shade
  • Soil: Moist, well-drained
  • Hardiness: Fully hardy, perfect for the Irish climate
  • Uses: Screening, structure, year-round interest, wildlife-friendly
Description:
With its elegant, multi-stemmed form and lustrous, evergreen leaves, the Nellie Stevens Holly brings a touch of timeless beauty to any garden. This holly is prized for its dense, upright habit and rich, glossy foliage—each leaf edged with gentle spines, catching the light on even the dullest winter day. In spring, delicate white flowers appear, quietly inviting bees and pollinators. By autumn, the branches are adorned with clusters of brilliant red berries, a feast for garden birds and a festive highlight for your outdoor space.
Ideal as a natural screen, focal point, or architectural accent, this Ilex is wonderfully low-maintenance and thrives in both sun and part shade. Its resilience makes it a steadfast choice for Irish gardens, providing structure and colour through every season.
Garden Notebook:
  • Planting Tip: Dig a generous hole, enrich with well-rotted compost, and water in well.
  • Care: Mulch annually, trim lightly in spring to shape.
  • Companion Planting: Pairs beautifully with Cornus, Viburnum, or underplanted with shade-loving hellebores.
Why We Love It:
A true garden classic, ‘Nellie Stevens’ holly is all about year-round interest and easy charm—perfect for creating privacy, shelter, or simply a little winter magic.
price_html: €880.00 – €1,250.00Price range: €880.00 through €1,250.00

name: Pittosporum Tom Thumb
description:
Rich in colour and full of character, Pittosporum ‘Tom Thumb’ is a compact evergreen shrub that brings depth, contrast, and structure to the garden all year round. Its small, glossy leaves emerge green before deepening into beautiful shades of bronze, purple, and chocolate-burgundy, making it a brilliant choice for adding darker foliage to a planting scheme.
Naturally neat and rounded in habit, it’s ideal for gardeners who want strong shape and colour without a lot of maintenance.
Why we love it
‘Tom Thumb’ is one of those plants that instantly adds drama in the loveliest, most understated way. Its dark foliage makes surrounding greens, silvers, and softer flowering plants stand out beautifully, and it works just as well in contemporary gardens as it does in more relaxed mixed borders.
Where it works best
Perfect for containers, front-of-border planting, gravel gardens, courtyards, and low-maintenance schemes, this compact shrub enjoys a sunny, sheltered spot with well-drained soil. It’s especially useful where you want evergreen structure with a richer, moodier colour palette.
What to expect
  • Compact evergreen shrub with rounded habit
  • Glossy foliage in deep purple-bronze tones
  • Excellent year-round colour and structure
  • Low maintenance and easy to grow
  • Great for pots, borders, and modern planting schemes
Caragh’s Garden Notebook
For the deepest leaf colour, plant ‘Tom Thumb’ in full sun. It pairs beautifully with grasses, silver foliage, soft pinks, and airy perennials, where its darker tones help create contrast and balance. A light trim in spring will keep it looking neat and encourage fresh new growth.
price_html: €12.50 – €30.00Price range: €12.50 through €30.00

add_to_cart.description: Select options for “Pittosporum Tom Thumb”

name: Pittosporum Golf Ball
description:
A neat little evergreen with beautiful natural shape, Pittosporum ‘Golf Ball’ is one of those plants that quietly makes everything around it look better. With its soft, rounded form and fresh green foliage, it brings structure, calm, and year-round interest to pots, borders, and contemporary planting schemes.
Perfect for adding shape without fuss, this compact shrub keeps its tidy mound with very little clipping, making it ideal for busy gardeners who still want that polished, designed look.
Why we love it
Pittosporum ‘Golf Ball’ is wonderfully versatile. It works beautifully in formal gardens, modern spaces, or mixed borders where you need an evergreen anchor among looser, more seasonal planting. Its naturally rounded habit gives all the charm of clipped topiary, but with a much softer, more relaxed feel.
Where it works best
This is a brilliant choice for containers, front door pots, gravel gardens, courtyards, and sunny borders. It loves a sheltered spot and is especially useful where you want year-round structure without something too heavy or rigid.
What to expect
  • Compact, rounded evergreen shrub
  • Fresh green foliage all year
  • Low maintenance and naturally tidy
  • Great for pots and small garden spaces
  • Ideal for modern or Mediterranean-style planting
Caragh’s Garden Notebook
For the best shape and colour, plant Pittosporum ‘Golf Ball’ in a sunny, sheltered position with free-draining soil. It’s perfect for framing entrances or repeating through a border to create rhythm and balance. In colder areas, container planting makes it easier to move to a protected spot in harsh weather.
price_html: €12.50 – €35.00Price range: €12.50 through €35.00

add_to_cart.description: Select options for “Pittosporum Golf Ball”

name: Pittosporum tenuifolium ‘Elizabeth’
description: Pittosporum ‘Elisabeth’ is a dense evergreen shrub with a neat habit, growing upright into a tall column. It has variegated grey-green foliage that are fringed with cream and vibrant pink. Tiny inconspicuous purple flowers are produced in late spring/early summer that have a slight honey-like fragrance. Very tolerant of coastal winds and sea-salt exposure, Pittosporum can make a fantastic hedge in seaside gardens. In gardens more inland, it will grow best in a sunny, sheltered site, although it will also tolerate semi-shade. Very easy to prune and maintain at any desired size, it can make a stunning addition to any garden, while also being suitable for pots and planters. Height and spread 3-4m approx.
price_html: €12.50 – €25.00Price range: €12.50 through €25.00

name: Photinia Red Robin
description:
Quick Facts
  • Common Name: Red Robin, Fire Thorn, Christmas Berry
  • Botanical Name: Photinia × fraseri ‘Red Robin’
  • Plant Type: Evergreen shrub/hedging plant
  • Mature Height: 3-4 metres
  • Mature Spread: 2-3 metres
  • Flowering Period: April-May
  • Flower Colour: Creamy white clusters
  • Foliage: Glossy green with brilliant red new growth
  • Hardiness: RHS H5 (-15°C to -10°C)
  • Soil Requirements: Well-drained, fertile soil
  • Aspect: Full sun to partial shade
  • Maintenance: Low to moderate
Description
Like nature’s own firework display, Photinia Red Robin ignites the Irish garden with its spectacular crimson new growth that emerges like flames against glossy evergreen foliage. This remarkable shrub transforms throughout the seasons, offering year-round structure whilst celebrating spring’s arrival with the most breathtaking display of ruby-red shoots that gradually mature to lustrous dark green.
The magic unfolds as clusters of creamy white flowers dance across the canopy in late spring, creating a stunning contrast against the vibrant foliage. Each pruning encourages fresh flushes of that signature scarlet growth, making this one of Ireland’s most beloved hedging plants for those seeking both privacy and year-round colour. The dense, upright habit creates perfect screening whilst the glossy leaves catch and reflect light beautifully throughout our changeable Irish weather.
This hardy hybrid thrives in our Irish climate, tolerating coastal winds and urban pollution with remarkable resilience. Whether planted as a statement specimen, formal hedge, or backdrop to softer perennials, Red Robin brings architectural strength to any garden design. Its vigorous growth and reliable nature make it a favourite amongst Irish gardeners who appreciate both beauty and practicality.
Perfect companions include silver-leaved lavender, purple-flowered hebe, or delicate ornamental grasses that complement rather than compete with its bold presence. In Irish cottage gardens, it pairs beautifully with climbing roses and clematis, whilst in contemporary designs it provides the perfect evergreen structure for seasonal plantings to dance around.
Caragh’s Garden Notebook
Plant Red Robin in well-drained soil enriched with compost, spacing individual plants 60-80cm apart for hedging or allowing 2-3 metres for specimen planting. Dig holes twice the width of the root ball and plant at the same depth as in the container. Water thoroughly after planting and mulch around the base to retain moisture.
For hedging, trim 2-3 times during the growing season (May to September) to encourage dense growth and maintain that stunning red flush. The first trim should be light, removing just the tips to encourage bushiness. Avoid hard pruning in winter as this can reduce flowering and leave plants vulnerable to frost damage.
Feed annually in early spring with a balanced fertiliser, and water regularly during dry spells, particularly in the first two years after planting. Red Robin responds beautifully to regular feeding, rewarding you with more intense red colouring on new growth. In containers, use a soil-based compost and ensure excellent drainage whilst maintaining consistent moisture levels.

name: Carex ‘Bronze Form’ (Sedge)
description:
Carex ‘Bronze Form’ is one of those plants that quietly makes everything around it look better. It forms a neat, arching mound of fine, bronze-copper foliage that moves beautifully in the breeze and adds warm colour all year round.
It’s perfect for softening the edge of a path, weaving through borders, underplanting shrubs, or adding texture in pots. Use it in drifts for a natural look, or repeat it throughout a scheme to tie planting together.

Why you’ll love it

  • Rich bronze foliage for year-round colour
  • Forms a tidy, arching clump with great texture
  • Brilliant for pots, borders, and underplanting
  • Low maintenance and easy to combine with other plants
  • Adds movement and softness to planting schemes

Position & care

  • Position: Sun or partial shade
  • Soil: Moist but well-drained soil (tolerates a range once established)
  • Hardiness: Hardy in most gardens; shelter from very harsh, exposed sites
  • Watering: Water well in the first season and during dry spells

Notes

Simply comb through or trim off any tired leaves in late winter/early spring to keep it looking fresh.

add_to_cart.description: Select options for “Carex ‘Bronze Form’ (Sedge)”

name: 7ft x 5ft Apex Roof Shed – Painted Finish
description:
The “just right” shed — beautifully finished.
If you want a shed that’s compact enough to suit most gardens, but spacious enough to be genuinely useful, the 7ft x 5ft Apex Roof Shed is a brilliant middle ground. It’s the ideal size for everyday storage — tools, mower, pots, cushions, kids’ outdoor gear — without taking over the garden.
Finished in a handcrafted painted exterior, it instantly looks more considered and design-led than a standard timber shed. It’s practical, tidy, and quietly lovely — the kind of garden building that feels like it belongs.
Shown here in Unveiled, a soft pale neutral pink that brings a gentle warmth and elegance to the garden (especially gorgeous with grasses, lavender, and white blooms).

Quick Facts

Size: 7ft x 5ft (approx. 2.13m x 1.52m)
Roof Style: Traditional apex roof for extra headroom and reliable rain run-off
Finish: Painted exterior (over treated timber)
Light: Side window for natural daylight
Access: Sturdy single door with secure ironmongery
Guarantee: 10-year guarantee

Choose Your Colour (6 Countryside Shades)

  • Callan – a lovely blue, fresh and uplifting
  • Unveiled – a pale neutral pink, soft and sophisticated
  • Schoolroom Green – classic heritage green, timeless in any garden
  • Mussel – a really lovely mid chalky blue, calm and coastal
  • Four Arches – a deep dark grey, verging on anthracite (modern and architectural)
  • Wild Raspberry – rich, joyful berry tones for a confident statement

Why You’ll Love It

A genuinely useful size
Big enough for the awkward items (mower, strimmer, folding chairs), but still neat and manageable in most gardens.
Classic apex roof = better internal storage
Extra height inside makes it easier to add shelving, hang tools, and keep the floor clear.
Painted finish that elevates the space
A painted shed feels intentional — it complements planting schemes and outdoor styling, rather than looking like an afterthought.
Natural light built in
The side window makes day-to-day use easier (especially in winter), and helps the shed feel bright and welcoming when you pop in for tools.

Perfect For

  • Lawn mower, strimmer, and garden tools
  • Pots, compost, seed trays, and plant supports
  • Outdoor cushions and furniture covers
  • Bikes and scooters (depending on handlebar width)
  • Children’s outdoor toys and sports gear

Caragh Garden Notebook

For a shed this size, a simple setup makes it feel twice as big: one shelf along the back wall, hooks behind the door, and a couple of labelled boxes for “small but important” things (twine, plant labels, batteries, bulbs). Colour-wise, Unveiled is a gorgeous choice if you want something soft and uplifting — it looks especially lovely against evergreen hedging.
Delivery & Installation Options
Delivery: Included throughout Ireland
Installation Option 1: Professional assembly on your existing prepared surface – €350
Requires a level, stable base such as concrete, paving slabs, or existing timber deck
Installation Option 2: Complete installation including crushed pebble base – €650 total
We lay a professional-grade base using crushed pebble set in honeycomb matting for superior drainage and long-term stability – the gold standard for timber structures in the Irish climate
Electrical Pre-Wiring: Available on request – we can install internal wiring ready for your electrician to connect to your mains supply. Please enquire for pricing.

name: Ficus carica Brown Turkey (Fig Tree)
description:
Ficus carica, better known as the common fig tree, is the tree species that produces the famous green, black or purple figs.

Ficus carica requires  well-drained soil.
Ficus carica is indifferent to soil type, but development is slower in poor and rocky soil.
A warm and sunny location is almost mandatory if you’re hoping for figs. Consider planting your trees near a wall, since the added protection will help your plants withstand the cold and help to ripen the fruit. Even without the fruit this tree is a beauty with wide open branches and large, lime green floppy leaves, it makes a beautiful specimen.
Exposure – full sun
Foliage – deciduous

Fruit formation – May to September.
Harvest – July to September

Planting, care and pruning will greatly help increase the quality of the fig harvest.

add_to_cart.description: Select options for “Ficus carica Brown Turkey (Fig Tree)”

name: Dryopteris wallichiana
description:
Dryopteris wallichiana is a brilliant fern for bringing structure and calm to shadier parts of the garden. It forms a handsome, upright clump of arching fronds with a slightly glossy finish and warm, golden-brown stems — giving it a richer, more “designed” look than many softer woodland ferns.
It’s ideal for underplanting trees and shrubs, filling borders in dappled shade, or adding texture alongside hostas, hellebores and spring bulbs. Once established, it’s a reliable, low-fuss perennial that comes back stronger each year.

Why you’ll love it

  • Elegant upright, arching fronds with great structure
  • Beautiful golden-brown stems for extra detail
  • Perfect for shade and woodland-style planting
  • Excellent for underplanting and adding texture
  • Hardy and dependable year after year

Position & care

  • Position: Partial to full shade (dappled shade is ideal)
  • Soil: Moist, humus-rich, well-drained soil
  • Hardiness: Very hardy
  • Watering: Keep watered in the first season; don’t let it dry out in summer

Caragh Nursery Notebook

A mulch of leaf mould or compost in spring will keep it happy and help it bulk up beautifully over time.

add_to_cart.description: Select options for “Dryopteris wallichiana”

name: Dryopteris affinis Cristata The King
description:
Quick Facts

  • Foliage: Evergreen to semi-evergreen fern with crested, “crowned” frond tips
  • Position: Partial shade to shade (best in dappled shade)
  • Soil: Moist but free-draining; humus-rich soil ideal
  • Habit: Strong, clump-forming fern with an upright, architectural shape
  • Season of interest: Year-round structure, with fresh new fronds in spring
  • Great for: Woodland gardens, shady borders, under trees, fern planting schemes
  • Wildlife: Excellent cover and texture for naturalistic planting

Why you’ll love it

If you want a fern with real presence, Dryopteris affinis ‘Cristata The King’ is exactly that. It’s bold and upright, with handsome fronds that often finish in distinctive crested tips — like a little crown — giving it a more sculptural look than many softer ferns.
It brings instant maturity to shady planting, filling that tricky “nothing wants to grow here” space with confident green structure. Beautiful with hellebores, hostas, epimediums and spring bulbs, and especially effective when repeated in a drift.

Caragh Garden Notebook (our growing tips)

Plant in dappled shade with moist, humus-rich soil for the best results. Work plenty of leaf mould or well-rotted compost into the planting hole, and keep it watered in dry spells during its first season.
In late winter or early spring, simply tidy away any tired or weathered fronds to make room for the fresh new growth. Avoid very dry, exposed spots — this is a fern that loves a bit of shelter and consistent moisture (without being waterlogged).
price_html: €9.50 – €45.00Price range: €9.50 through €45.00

name: Malus ‘Coccinella’ / Crab Apple
description:
Malus floribunda ‘Coccinella’, also known as Coccinella Crab Apple  or Malus ‘Courtarou’, is a form of Japanese Crab Apple that produces masses of deep pink flowers in late spring, that are later followed by small red to orange fruits in the autumn, which persist into the winter. In early spring, the leaves emeerge purple-red in colour, before maturing to deep green over the summer. In autumn, leaves will turn yellow to orange before falling.

A very attractive tree with many wildlife benefits, and a nice compact ultimate size of 4-6m with a spread of 3-4m that will work beautifully in gardens of any size.
price_html: €150.00 – €210.00Price range: €150.00 through €210.00

add_to_cart.description: Select options for “Malus ‘Coccinella’ / Crab Apple”

name: Kubu “Village Range” Planters (Fully Lined)
description:
Soft, oversized and beautifully timeless — our Kubu Village Range planters are made for simple, confident planting. We first used this exact style in a well-known local retail village, where the brief was clear: create impact that feels natural, calm, and design-led. These baskets did it effortlessly.
The weave is beautifully soft with a relaxed, lived-in texture that suits everything from modern courtyards to country doorways. And because each planter is fully lined, they’re ready to plant up and style straight away.
Our favourite way to use them: keep it simple — one variety per pot, repeated. It’s the easiest way to get that “professionally planted” look with very little fuss.

Available sizes (diameter)

  • 120cm
  • 100cm
  • 80cm
  • 60cm
  • 45cm

Planting ideas we love

Choose one plant and let it fill the space:
  • Viburnum opulus (a personal favourite — soft, generous, and full of charm)
  • Viburnum carlesii or Viburnum ‘Charles Lamont’ for scented spring flowering
  • A cloud of Nepeta for long-lasting colour and movement
  • Agapanthus for summer structure and elegance
  • Low pines for evergreen simplicity
  • Or keep it natural and seasonal with hellebores or ornamental grasses

Practical notes

  • Fully lined for a neat finish and practical
  • For best results, ensure good drainage (add drainage material at the base and avoid leaving pots sitting in water)
  • A sheltered spot helps keep them looking their best over time
price_html: €70.00 – €350.00Price range: €70.00 through €350.00

add_to_cart.description: Select options for “Kubu “Village Range” Planters (Fully Lined)”

name: Lavanduler angustifolia ‘White Summer’
description:
Fresh, bright and wonderfully fragrant, Lavandula angustifolia ‘White Summer’ is a beautiful way to bring a softer, lighter feel to borders and pots. Its clean white flower spikes rise above silvery-green foliage through summer, adding structure, scent, and that timeless Mediterranean look — while still being a brilliant performer in Irish gardens when given the right spot.
It’s ideal for edging paths, planting in gravel, or pairing with roses, salvias and ornamental grasses for a relaxed, sun-loving scheme. And of course, bees absolutely adore it.

Quick Facts

  • Botanical name: Lavandula angustifolia ‘White Summer’
  • Plant type: Evergreen shrub (English lavender)
  • Flowers: White, fragrant, summer flowering
  • Foliage: Silvery-green, aromatic
  • Position: Full sun
  • Soil: Free-draining / well-drained (avoid heavy, wet soil)
  • Hardiness: Hardy in Irish gardens with good drainage
  • Height x Spread: Approx. 40–60cm x 40–60cm
  • Wildlife: Excellent for bees and pollinators
  • Best for: Edging, pots, gravel gardens, coastal gardens, white/green planting schemes

Caragh Garden Notebook

White flowers in summer planting are like a breath of fresh air — they lift everything around them and make the garden feel calmer. ‘White Summer’ does that beautifully, with the added bonus of that unmistakable lavender scent every time you walk past.
We love it planted in a sunny line along a path, where it can be brushed by sleeves and little hands, or in pots by the door where the fragrance greets you before you even step outside. It’s simple, timeless, and quietly joyful.
Caragh tip: Lavender loves a haircut. Trim after flowering to keep it compact and long-lived — but avoid cutting back into old woody stems.

images.name: Lavender fields in Selbourne, Hampshire, England in Summer

add_to_cart.description: Select options for “Lavanduler angustifolia ‘White Summer’”

name: Prunus serrula Multi-stem (Tibetan peeling bark Cherry)
description:
Prunus serrula is a tree you choose for year-round beauty — not just a few weeks of flower. Its real magic is the bark: a rich, glossy mahogany-copper that catches the light, especially in winter when the garden needs it most. In spring it produces small, delicate white flowers, followed by fresh green leaves through summer and warm autumn colour as the season turns.
Grown as a multi-stem, Prunus serrula becomes even more sculptural, with several trunks showing off that polished bark from every angle. It’s an ideal feature tree near a path, patio, or window where you’ll enjoy it up close.

Why you’ll love it

  • Stunning shiny copper-red bark for winter interest
  • Multi-stem form gives extra structure and character
  • Pretty white spring blossom
  • Lovely autumn colour before leaf fall
  • Perfect feature tree for small to medium gardens

Position & care

  • Position: Full sun to partial shade (best bark colour in sun)
  • Soil: Fertile, well-drained soil; avoid waterlogged ground
  • Hardiness: Very hardy
  • Watering: Water regularly in the first year while it establishes

Notes

This is a great tree to underplant with spring bulbs or woodland-style perennials, so you get a full season of interest around it.

images.name: Tree blossom blooming in spring at Nigel Dunnet Beech Gardens March April garden on the Barbican Estate in the City of London UK 2022   KATHY DEWITT

add_to_cart.description: Select options for “Prunus serrula Multi-stem (Tibetan peeling bark Cherry)”

name: Prunus serrulata ‘Royal Burgundy’ Multi-stem (Flowering Cherry)
description:
Prunus ‘Royal Burgundy’ is a real statement tree — loved for its rich, dark foliage and its soft pink spring blossom. In early to mid-spring, the branches are dotted with clusters of pink flowers, and as the season moves on the leaves deepen into a glossy burgundy-purple that holds its colour beautifully through summer.
As a multi-stem, it has a more sculptural, airy shape than a standard tree — perfect if you want something with presence and personality, without blocking light. It’s stunning in a lawn, as a feature near a patio, or used to add contrast among fresh greens and silvery planting.

Why you’ll love it

  • Pink spring blossom with real seasonal impact
  • Deep burgundy-purple foliage from spring through summer
  • Multi-stem form for a natural, architectural shape
  • Great contrast tree for modern or cottage-style gardens
  • Ideal focal point for small to medium gardens

Position & care

  • Position: Full sun for best leaf colour and flowering (light shade tolerated)
  • Soil: Well-drained, reasonably fertile soil
  • Hardiness: Very hardy
  • Watering: Water regularly in the first year while establishing

Notes

Avoid planting in very wet ground. A mulch in spring helps retain moisture and supports strong growth.

images.name: Ornamental cherry (Prunus serrulata ‘Royal Burgundy’), BS Saemann, Federal Republic of Germany

add_to_cart.description: Select options for “Prunus serrulata ‘Royal Burgundy’ Multi-stem (Flowering Cherry)”

name: Liquidambar styraciflua Worplesdon specimen multistem
description:
If you’re looking for a tree that earns its space, Liquidambar ‘Worplesdon’ is hard to beat — especially for autumn colour. Through spring and summer it carries fresh green, star-shaped leaves, building a lovely, full canopy. Then in autumn it turns into a proper show: glowing shades of yellow, orange, crimson and deep red, often all on the same tree.
In multi-stem form, it has a more natural, architectural shape, with multiple trunks creating instant structure and a softer outline — ideal as a feature tree in a lawn, at the edge of a garden room, or as part of a bigger planting scheme where you want height and seasonal drama.

Why you’ll love it

  • Outstanding autumn colour (yellow, orange, red, burgundy)
  • Distinctive star-shaped foliage
  • Multi-stem structure for year-round shape and presence
  • A beautiful feature tree for lawns and larger borders
  • Hardy and reliable once established

Position & care

  • Position: Full sun to light shade (best colour in sun)
  • Soil: Moist but well-drained soil; prefers slightly acidic to neutral
  • Hardiness: Very hardy
  • Watering: Water well in the first year and during dry spells

Notes

Liquidambar can be slower to settle in very chalky or dry soils. If your soil is alkaline, adding organic matter and mulching annually will help it thrive.

**The colour in this image depicts the autumn foliage**

add_to_cart.description: Select options for “Liquidambar styraciflua Worplesdon specimen multistem”

name: Crataegus ‘Paul’s Scarlet’ Multi-stem / Red Hawthorn Multistem
description:
Crataegus ‘Paul’s Scarlet’ is one of those trees that quietly earns its keep all year, then absolutely steals the show in late spring. Grown as a multi-stem, it has a naturally architectural shape — perfect for adding structure to a border, softening a boundary, or creating a focal point in a smaller garden without feeling heavy.
In May and June, it’s covered in rich, double, deep pink-red flowers that look almost like tiny roses scattered through the branches. As the season moves on, the foliage stays a fresh, healthy green before turning warm tones in autumn.
A brilliant choice if you want something hardy, reliable, and full of character — especially in Irish gardens.

Why you’ll love it

  • Masses of double, deep pink-red flowers in late spring
  • Multi-stem form for instant shape and presence
  • Hardy and wind-tolerant once established
  • Great for pollinators when in flower
  • Works beautifully in mixed borders, lawns, and entrance planting

Position & care

  • Position: Full sun to partial shade
  • Soil: Most well-drained garden soils
  • Hardiness: Very hardy
  • Watering: Water well in the first year while it establishes

Notes

Like many hawthorns, it can have thorns — so it’s best placed away from narrow paths or play areas.

images.name: Crataegus Pauls Scarlet Tree. Flowering branch of Crataegus

add_to_cart.description: Select options for “Crataegus ‘Paul’s Scarlet’ Multi-stem / Red Hawthorn Multistem”

name: Pinus sylvestris Pom Pom
description:
Quick Facts
  • Common Name: Scots Pine, Scotch Pine
  • Botanical Name: Pinus sylvestris
  • Plant Type: Evergreen conifer tree
  • Mature Height: 15-25m
  • Mature Spread: 6-10m
  • Flowering Period: May (cones develop)
  • Flower Colour: Yellow male cones, green female cones
  • Foliage: Blue-green needles in pairs, twisted
  • Hardiness: RHS H7 (very hardy)
  • Soil Requirements: Well-drained, tolerates poor and acidic soils
  • Aspect: Full sun
  • Maintenance: Low
Description
Discover the timeless beauty of Pinus sylvestris, the iconic Scots Pine that brings native character, year-round structure, and distinctive architectural presence to your garden with its striking blue-green foliage and magnificent orange-red bark. This outstanding native conifer offers exceptional ornamental value—distinctive twisted needles in blue-green tones that create soft texture throughout the seasons, a naturally picturesque form that becomes increasingly characterful with age, and spectacular flaking bark that reveals warm orange-red tones beneath, creating stunning winter interest that glows in low sunlight.
Throughout the year, this captivating tree displays its characteristic paired needles of blue-green to grey-green, each measuring 4-7cm in length and distinctively twisted, creating a soft, feathery appearance that sets this apart from other pines. The naturally irregular branching structure develops a flat-topped crown with age, creating the iconic silhouette associated with Scottish Highland landscapes. In spring, clusters of yellow male cones release pollen, whilst small green female cones develop into attractive grey-brown cones that persist on the tree. The bark is the true showstopper—on mature trees, the deeply fissured orange-red to cinnamon bark creates spectacular visual interest, particularly striking in winter when low sun illuminates the warm tones.
Native to Scotland and widely naturalized across Ireland and northern Europe, this remarkable conifer is Britain and Ireland’s only native pine species, with ancient Caledonian pine forests representing some of Europe’s most important woodland ecosystems. Exceptionally hardy and adaptable, Scots Pine thrives in Irish conditions, tolerating poor soils, exposure, coastal winds, drought, and urban pollution with remarkable resilience. The robust nature and wildlife value make this an invaluable choice for naturalistic plantings and ecological gardens.
Create stunning compositions by planting as specimen trees in large gardens, estates, or parkland where their picturesque form and bark can be fully appreciated. Magnificent in naturalistic woodland plantings, shelter belts, or groups for screening and wildlife habitat. Works beautifully combined with native birch, rowan, and oak for authentic Irish woodland character, or underplanted with heathers, bilberry, and native grasses that thrive in similar acidic, free-draining conditions. Excellent for wildlife gardens, providing habitat and food for red squirrels, crossbills, and numerous other species.
Caragh Garden Notebook
Planting: Space trees 8-10m apart for groupings or woodland plantings, or allow 10-12m for specimen placement. Plant container-grown specimens year-round, though autumn or early spring is ideal. Dig holes twice the width of the root ball but no deeper—pines prefer shallow planting. Do not incorporate organic matter as this species thrives in lean, poor conditions. Plant at the same depth as the container. Stake for the first 2 years if exposed. Water thoroughly initially but avoid overwatering.
Soil Preparation: Thrives in well-drained soil with pH 4.5-7.0. Exceptionally tolerant of poor, sandy, stony, and acidic soils. Prefers lean, free-draining conditions—avoid rich, moisture-retentive soils which can cause soft growth and disease. One of the best conifers for acidic heathland and sandy soils. Tolerates drought, exposure, and coastal conditions. Avoid only waterlogged sites. No soil improvement necessary—this pine performs best in challenging, nutrient-poor conditions.
Container Growing: Not suitable for long-term container growing due to size and extensive root system. Young specimens can be grown temporarily as bonsai or in very large containers for several years, but standard trees should be planted out within 2-3 years for best long-term health and to achieve full size, characteristic bark development, and picturesque form.
Seasonal Care: Requires minimal maintenance—naturally develops characterful form without pruning. Remove only dead or damaged branches as needed. Can be lightly shaped when young by pinching back new growth (candles) in spring by half to encourage denser growth and more compact form, though this is optional. Apply no fertiliser—lean conditions produce the best, most resilient growth and enhance bark colour. Water only during establishment or prolonged drought in the first 2 years. Once established, extremely drought-tolerant and requires no supplementary care.
Propagation: Propagate from seed collected from mature cones in autumn. Stratify seeds for 4-6 weeks before sowing in spring in free-draining, acidic compost. Germination can be slow and variable. Seedlings show natural variation in form and needle colour. Take many years to reach maturity. Most gardeners prefer to purchase nursery-grown specimens for guaranteed quality and faster establishment.
This magnificent native beauty brings such wonderful character to Irish gardens—that distinctive blue-green twisted foliage and especially that stunning orange-red bark on mature trees are absolutely breathtaking! Tough as nails, thrives in poor soils, and provides invaluable wildlife habitat. A true icon of our native landscape that becomes more picturesque and characterful with every passing year!

name: Prunus Kanzan Specimen Multistem
description:
runus ‘Kanzan’ is one of the most loved flowering cherries for a reason — it gives you that proper spring moment. In April and May, the bare branches burst into big, ruffled clusters of double pink blossom, and as the flowers fade the fresh bronze-red leaves emerge, maturing to green through summer.
In multi-stem form, ‘Kanzan’ has extra presence and a softer, more natural silhouette — ideal as a focal point in a lawn, at the edge of a patio, or as part of a layered planting scheme where you want height, light, and seasonal drama without heaviness.

Why you’ll love it

  • Show-stopping double pink blossom in spring
  • Multi-stem shape for instant structure and impact
  • New foliage emerges bronze-red, then turns green
  • Great for pollinators in spring
  • A brilliant feature tree for small to medium gardens

Position & care

  • Position: Full sun is best (light shade is fine)
  • Soil: Fertile, well-drained soil; avoid waterlogged ground
  • Hardiness: Very hardy
  • Watering: Keep well-watered in the first year to establish

Notes

Flowering cherries prefer a sheltered spot away from harsh, drying winds if possible, especially in more exposed gardens.

name: Dunmore Modular Sofa Set
description:
For big, beautiful lounging — made to fit your space.
The Dunmore Modular Sofa Set takes everything we love about the original Dunmore — those gently curved teak lines and the soft, natural vanilla Olefin cushions — and brings it into a brand-new modular layout that’s made for stretching out properly.
With a 3-seat left arm, 3-seat right arm, and a generous corner element, this is the kind of outdoor seating that turns a patio into a living room (only with better light and birdsong). It’s calm, timeless, and quietly luxurious — perfect for family gatherings, summer evenings with friends, or simply claiming a corner of the garden as your own.
Finished with two interlocking coffee tables, you can keep things layered and styled, or split them apart when you’re entertaining.
What’s Included
1 x 3-seat left arm section
1 x 3-seat right arm section
1 x corner element
2 x interlocking coffee tables
Materials & Colour
Curved teak frame
Cushions in vanilla / natural cream Olefin fabric
Why We Love It
Modular format gives you that “built-in” look, without the fuss
Soft neutral cushions that work with any garden style — from Mediterranean to cottage planting
Two nesting/interlocking tables for flexible entertaining and everyday ease
If you want one outdoor set that makes your garden feel like a destination, this is it.
